quick_skim.title: Lyapunov-Guided Training for Hardware-Safe Neural Networks Under Fixed-Point Arithmetic
quick_skim.abstract: Low-precision neural networks are attractive for resource-constrained hardware, but fixed-point arithmetic introduces failure modes that are often hidden by idealised quantisation models. In particular, two's-complement overflow wrapping can corrupt hidden activations by changing both their magnitude and sign, leading to unstable numerical error propagation and severe accuracy degradation. This paper proposes a Lyapunov-stabilised quantisation framework for low-precision neural networks operating under hardware-style wrapping arithmetic. The hidden-state energy is monitored through a layerwise Lyapunov function, and a monotone projection is applied to enforce bounded and non-increasing state evolution across depth. The method is evaluated on MNIST using a compact patch-based transformer under post-training quantisation and quantisation-aware training with fixed-point bit-widths from 4 to 16 bits. Monte Carlo results show that unconstrained wrapped quantisation-aware training collapses to near-chance accuracy across 6-16 bits, with activation overflow rates exceeding 11%. In contrast, the proposed monotone Lyapunov projection suppresses activation overflow to below 0.012% and restores stable low-precision learning, achieving 86.55% accuracy at 12 bits. These results demonstrate that Lyapunov-based state control can act as a hardware-aware stabilisation mechanism for reliable fixed-point neural inference and training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes Lyapunov-stabilized quantization for hardware-efficient low-precision neural networks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出李雅普诺夫稳定量化方法，用于硬件高效的低精度神经网络
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出李雅普诺夫稳定量化方法，用于硬件高效的低精度神经网络
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出李雅普诺夫稳定量化方法，用于硬件高效的低精度神经网络
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work introduces a Lyapunov-guided training framework to ensure stability of low-precision neural networks under fixed-point arithmetic, enabling efficient hardware deployment relevant to communication devices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对低精度神经网络在定点算术下因溢出导致的不稳定性，提出李雅普诺夫稳定量化框架。通过逐层监控隐状态能量并施加单调投影，确保前向传播有界且非增。该方法可在不显著影响精度的情况下实现高效硬件推理，适用于通信系统中资源受限的神经网络部署，如均衡器和信道估计器。

quick_skim.title: A Hardware-Aware Open-Source Framework for Design Space Exploration of Mixed-Signal Spiking Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Energy-efficient neuromorphic computing at the edge requires simulation tools that can capture the non-ideal behavior of mixed-signal spiking neural network (SNN) hardware while supporting system-level design exploration. This work presents an open-source hardware-aware simulation framework for mixed-signal SNNs that enables comparative analysis across neuron, synapse and architecture choices. The framework supports multiple neuron models, including Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (LIF), Hodgkin-Huxley (HH) and Axon-Hillock (AH), together with non-volatile analog synapses based on floating-gate transistors and ReRAM devices. By incorporating device-level nonlinearities directly into PyTorch-based training and inference, the tool enables optimization of physical synaptic parameters rather than idealized abstract weights. The framework is evaluated on standard neuromorphic benchmarks, including N-MNIST, DVS Gesture and Spiking Heidelberg Digits (SHD). For each model dataset configuration, it reports classification accuracy together with hardware-oriented metrics such as silicon area, power consumption and quantization sensitivity. These capabilities enable cross-layer design space exploration and help identify neuron-synapse configurations that best satisfy application-specific constraints on accuracy, energy efficiency, area and hardware fidelity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: open-source framework for mixed-signal spiking neural networks, enabling efficient design space exploration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 混合信号脉冲神经网络的硬件感知仿真框架，支持架构探索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 混合信号脉冲神经网络的硬件感知仿真框架，支持架构探索

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a hardware-aware open-source simulation framework for mixed-signal SNNs, supporting multiple neuron models and analog synapses, enabling design space exploration for energy-efficient neuromorphic computing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一个开源硬件感知仿真框架，用于混合信号脉冲神经网络（SNN）的设计空间探索。框架支持LIF、HH等神经元模型和浮栅晶体管等非易失性模拟突触，将器件非理想性融入PyTorch训练。这一工具可加速高效能脉冲神经网络在边缘设备上的应用，也为通信系统的节能神经网络设计提供了潜在方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一个开源硬件感知仿真框架，用于混合信号脉冲神经网络（SNN）的设计空间探索。框架支持LIF、HH等神经元模型和浮栅晶体管等非易失性模拟突触，将器件非理想性融入PyTorch训练。这一工具可加速高效能脉冲神经网络在边缘设备上的应用，也为通信系统的节能神经网络设计提供了潜在方法。

quick_skim.title: Generative Communications: Overview, Technologies, and Trends
quick_skim.abstract: The groundbreaking development of generative artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly boosting the ability to generate content such as images and videos, reshaping communication paradigms. This article introduces generative communications (GenCom), a novel paradigm for 6G networks in which large AI models (LAMs) drive semantic understanding, reasoning, and content generation, embedding these into the communication process. Unlike traditional systems that strictly pursue accurate bit transmission, GenCom enables transmitters to convey only minimal yet sufficient information, while receivers leverage shared generative priors and knowledge bases to synthesize the intended output. Communication is thus redefined as controlled generation rather than data reproduction. We formalize the concept of GenCom, clarify its AI-native and generation-driven properties, and present its core mechanisms. A two-layer GenCom architecture supported by key enabling technologies is proposed, and analysis of four representative application scenarios demonstrates that GenCom offers ultra-efficient transmission, semantic-level robustness, and new network functions. Finally, we outline future research directions, including foundational theory and real-time processing, highlighting a promising pathway toward 6G networks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deep learning techniques applied to communications
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度学习技术应用于通信
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度学习技术应用于通信
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度学习技术应用于通信
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Survey on generative communications, where large AI models drive semantic understanding and content generation, redefining communication as controlled generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统通信追求精确比特传输，而生成式人工智能可重塑通信范式。本文综述生成式通信(GenCom)技术，提出在6G网络中利用大模型驱动语义理解、推理和内容生成，接收端通过共享生成先验合成输出。GenCom将通信定义为受控生成而非数据复制，有望极大提升频谱效率和用户体验。该综述为通信领域中深度学习应用提供了新视角，总结了关键技术、趋势与挑战。
